title: Build shared AWS demo environment with generic sample data
description: Create persistent AWS infrastructure that any V1 demo tenant can connect to. Deploy via CloudFormation/Terraform: (1) EC2 instances with Trend agents installed (Windows Server + Linux), (2) EKS cluster with sample containerized workloads, (3) S3 bucket with sample files/logs, (4) VPC with proper network topology. Pre-stage sample threats/detections for demo scenarios. Document how to connect a new V1 tenant to this environment.

substeps.title: Create shared AWS config module — single source of truth for resource names/ARNs
substeps.notes: infra/config.py (Python) + infra/config.js (JS/Chrome extension). Covers: region, account, S3 buckets, IAM role ARNs, Lambda names, CFN stack names, EKS cluster, EC2 names. Python module also supports `python infra/config.py > config.json` for other consumers.

substeps.title: CloudFormation: VPC with public/private subnets, IGW, NAT
CloudFormation: EC2 instances (Windows Server + Linux) with Trend agents
CloudFormation/eksctl: EKS cluster with sample vulnerable workloads
Stage sample threat data and log files in shared S3 bucket
Document how to connect a new V1 tenant to shared environment

tests: EC2 instances running with Trend agents reporting to a V1 tenant
EKS cluster has sample pods with known vulnerabilities for Container Security demo
A new V1 tenant can connect via Cloud Account and see the AWS resources within 5 minutes
Sample detections/alerts appear in V1 within 10 minutes of connection
Infrastructure survives instance stop/start (persistent, not ephemeral)
research_notes: Account ID 752266476357, region us-east-1, profile hackathon (from infra/README.md). This substep (aws-config) is the config module only — full CFN stacks are subsequent substeps. Config covers all resource names so other workstreams never hardcode values.
